title: China-to-Guatemala Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC — IEC 62920 and IEC 61000 Evidence for PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: Guatemala PV inverter EMC expectations should be confirmed against COGUANOR-adopted standards, CNEE or distributor project specifications, and procurement requirements. For internationally supplied grid-tied inverters, the practical evidence package commonly relies on IEC 62920 for photovoltaic power conversion equipment EMC and relevant IEC 61000 series tests, including harmonic current and immunity tests where applicable. Because Guatemala operates at 60 Hz and 120/240 V low-voltage service, EMC and harmonics documentation should identify the tested voltage, frequency, firmware, and model configuration.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility test and limit standards, including harmonic current emission and immunity standards where applicable
COGUANOR — Guatemalan national standards adoption and conformity context
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence is commonly tied to GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, and China domestic laboratory reports. These tests may use IEC 61000-family methods, but the certificate scope, limits, grid conditions, and report language may not match the IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 evidence expected by a Guatemala importer, distributor, CNEE project reviewer, or project owner. A China EMC certificate also typically reflects 50 Hz / 220-380 V configuration rather than Guatemala's 60 Hz / 120-240 V grid.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC requirements
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China GB/T EMC testing does not automatically demonstrate Guatemala-acceptable IEC EMC evidence. Exporters should prepare IEC 62920 and relevant IEC 61000 reports for the exact inverter model, power rating, firmware, and grid configuration. Where prior EMC or harmonic reports were tested only at Chinese grid conditions, confirm whether the importer, distributor, or project engineer requires retesting or engineering assessment at 60 Hz / 120-240 V.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent and often mandatory through COGUANOR conformity expectations, CNEE/distributor technical review, or procurement specifications for grid-tied PV equipment
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or certification-scheme dependent in China; domestic GB/T EMC evidence is not a Guatemala acceptance decision

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not rely on a China GB/T EMC certificate alone for Guatemala PV inverter projects. Build an IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 EMC file that names the exact model, firmware, voltage, and 60 Hz configuration, then confirm with the importer, COGUANOR conformity route, distributor, or project engineer whether supplementary testing is required.

rows.requirement: CNEE / Distributor Grid Connection — Anti-Islanding, IEC 61727 Interface, and 60 Hz / 120-240 V Settings
rows.target_requirement: Guatemala grid-tied PV inverters are reviewed in the context of CNEE technical norms, distributor interconnection practice, and project-owner requirements. For distributed renewable generation and self-consumption with exported surplus, the installer or project owner should confirm CNEE's current Generación Distribuida Renovable rules and the applicable distributor conditions before connection. Practical inverter evidence normally centers on IEC 62116 anti-islanding, IEC 61727 utility-interface behavior, and protection settings suitable for Guatemala's 60 Hz, 120/240 V low-voltage service. Larger projects may also need AMM or transmission/distribution operator review. Factory settings validated for China's 50 Hz, 220/380 V grid should not be used as Guatemala settings without reconfiguration and project-specific validation.
target_standards: CNEE — Norma Técnica para la Conexión, Operación, Control y Comercialización de la Generación Distribuida Renovable and current distributed renewable generation guidance
IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
Guatemala low-voltage grid context — 60 Hz, 120/240 V service; distributor-specific voltage and protection settings must be confirmed
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 for PV power-station grid connection, NB/T 32004 inverter technical specifications, GB/T 37408 inverter technical requirements, and domestic anti-islanding or protection test results. These documents are prepared for China's 50 Hz grid and standard 220/380 V low-voltage supply. They do not establish compliance with Guatemala CNEE or distributor interconnection requirements and do not prove that the inverter's protection thresholds, anti-islanding behavior, reconnection delays, or voltage ride-through settings are appropriate for a 60 Hz, 120/240 V grid.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese GB/T and NB/T grid documents do not satisfy Guatemala CNEE, distributor, or AMM project gates by themselves. Exporters should prepare IEC 62116 anti-islanding evidence, IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence, a Guatemala-specific protection settings table, and commissioning documentation showing 60 Hz / 120-240 V configuration. Confirm whether the project is distributed renewable generation, self-consumption with surplus, a distributor-interconnected project, or an AMM wholesale-market project; the applicable approval path changes the documentary package.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for grid-tied PV connection: CNEE rules, distributor conditions, and where applicable AMM or operator requirements are project gates before energisation
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China domestic grid review but does not transfer to Guatemala CNEE, distributor, or AMM acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Guatemala grid connection as a fresh CNEE and distributor or AMM review, not as an extension of China GB/T acceptance. Reconfigure and validate the inverter for Guatemala's 60 Hz / 120-240 V grid, with IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 evidence tied to the supplied model and firmware. Confirm the current CNEE rule, distributor settings, and any AMM requirements before shipment.

rows.requirement: Distributed Renewable Generation and Self-Consumption — CNEE, Distributor, and AMM Project Gates
rows.target_requirement: Guatemala permits distributed renewable generation and self-consumption arrangements, but the inverter is only one part of the approval package. The project owner normally needs to follow CNEE technical rules, the relevant distributor's interconnection process, metering requirements, protection review, and commissioning checks. Projects participating in the wholesale market or connected at higher voltage may involve AMM, INDE, distributor, or transmission-operator requirements. Chinese inverter documentation should therefore be packaged around the Guatemala project route, not only around product certification.
target_standards: CNEE — distributed renewable generation and self-consumption technical norms
Distributor interconnection and metering procedures — EEGSA, Energuate, or other applicable distributor
AMM market and operating requirements where the project participates in the wholesale electricity market
rows.china_equivalent: China distributed PV connection approvals are issued under Chinese energy policy, grid company rules, and domestic inverter standards. Those approvals may show that an inverter model has been used in Chinese distributed PV projects, but they do not address Guatemala's CNEE procedure, distributor metering and protection review, AMM rules, or the 60 Hz grid configuration.
china_standards: NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
rows.gap: Gap: A China domestic distributed PV approval does not transfer to Guatemala. The importer or EPC should map the project to the correct Guatemala route, obtain distributor or AMM feedback on inverter settings and documentation, and provide Spanish or bilingual technical files where required. Model certificates should be supplemented by project commissioning records, settings files, and evidence that the inverter is configured for Guatemala's 60 Hz network.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as a project approval path where a PV system exports to the distribution grid, participates in self-consumption with surplus, or enters the wholesale market
mandatory_status.cn: China distributed PV approvals are local project approvals and are not market-access approvals for Guatemala

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Before committing a Chinese inverter to a Guatemala distributed PV or self-consumption project, identify the CNEE, distributor, and possible AMM route and ask for the exact documentation and settings expected. China project approvals and GB/T certificates are useful background only; they do not replace Guatemala interconnection review or 60 Hz commissioning evidence.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — COGUANOR Conformity and Project Procurement Context
rows.target_requirement: For Guatemala market entry and grid-tied PV projects, inverter safety evidence should be checked against COGUANOR-adopted standards, importer conformity obligations, and project specifications. IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 are the international safety standards normally requested for PV power converters and inverters. COGUANOR is Guatemala's national standards body and may adopt or reference IEC-based standards, while CNEE, distributors, or project owners may require IEC 62109 reports as part of the equipment technical file. Evidence should cover the specific model or model family, firmware-relevant safety functions, nameplate ratings, environmental ratings, and installation manual.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
COGUANOR — Guatemalan national standards adoption and conformity context
rows.china_equivalent: China's common PV inverter safety evidence includes GB/T 37408-2019 and voluntary CQC or CGC certification using Chinese domestic standards and CNAS-accredited laboratories. Such evidence can be useful background, but it is not the same as IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certification and is not an automatic Guatemala conformity or project acceptance decision. The Chinese file may also assume China nameplate conventions, grid voltage, 50 Hz operation, and Chinese-language installation documentation.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: A Chinese GB/T 37408 or NB/T 32004 safety file does not replace IEC 62109-1/-2 evidence for Guatemala. Exporters should obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test reports or certificates for the supplied model or family, align labels and manuals with Spanish or bilingual project requirements, and verify whether COGUANOR conformity or the project owner requires a locally recognised certificate, accredited-lab report, or additional importer declaration.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent and often mandatory through COGUANOR conformity expectations, CNEE/distributor technical files, importer obligations, or procurement specifications
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408 and many CQC/CGC PV inverter certifications are China domestic or scheme-specific evidence, not Guatemala acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A China-only GB/T safety certificate is not enough to assume Guatemala acceptance. Prepare IEC 62109-1/-2 evidence for the actual inverter model, confirm COGUANOR conformity expectations and project-owner requirements, and make sure labels and manuals support Guatemala installation and inspection.
